message:
feat(autofix): add unattended repair mode, version manager recovery, and doctor-fix diagnostics with comprehensive test suites

Introduces three new autofix subsystems for handling installation recovery
scenarios that previously required manual intervention: unattended batch
repair, version manager (nvm/pyenv/rustup) state recovery, and a doctor
sub-command that diagnoses and fixes common installation health issues.

Unattended repair mode (autofix_unattended.sh):
- Add batch repair orchestrator that detects and fixes common installation
  problems without interactive prompts — designed for cron/systemd timer
  execution
- Repair capabilities: stale lock file detection and cleanup, orphaned
  backup directory pruning, interrupted migration recovery, version file
  consistency validation
- Each repair action is journaled through the existing autofix change
  tracking system with proper undo commands
- Add configuration for repair aggressiveness levels (conservative/moderate/
  aggressive) controlling which repairs are attempted automatically

Version manager recovery (autofix_version_managers.sh):
- Add detection and repair for nvm, pyenv, and rustup installations that
  have become corrupted or misconfigured after ACFS PATH modifications
- Detect broken shims, stale version symlinks, and missing shell integration
  hooks
- Repair by re-sourcing version manager init scripts and rebuilding shim
  directories
- All repairs journaled with rollback capability

Doctor diagnostics (doctor_fix.sh):
- Add comprehensive installation health check that validates PATH ordering,
  shell config integrity, binary accessibility, version file consistency,
  and state directory permissions
- Produce structured JSON diagnostic report with severity-tagged findings
- Auto-fix mode that applies safe repairs for issues categorized as
  auto-fixable, with manual intervention required for dangerous findings
- Integration with the autofix journaling system for all automated repairs

Test suites (3 new + 2 expanded):
- test_autofix_unattended.sh: tests for lock file cleanup, backup pruning,
  migration recovery, and version file repair across all aggressiveness levels
- test_autofix_version_managers.sh: tests for nvm/pyenv/rustup detection,
  shim rebuilding, and shell integration repair
- test_doctor_fix.sh: tests for diagnostic report generation, auto-fix
  application, and manual-intervention flagging
- Expanded test_autofix.sh with additional undo lifecycle and checksum tests
- Expanded test_changelog_export_status.sh with upgrade journal preservation
  tests for the new recovery paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+                --arg leftover_count "$(find "$TARGET_HOME" -maxdepth 1 -type d -name ".acfs-backup-*" | wc -l | tr -d " ")" \
+                --arg parent_synced "$(grep -Fx "dir:$TARGET_HOME" "$FSYNC_LOG_PATH" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo yes || echo no)" \

+    if printf '%s\n' "$output" | jq -e '

+        and .leftover_count == "0"
+        and .parent_synced == "yes"
+    ' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        harness_pass "autofix_existing backup cleans partial dir after copy failure"

+        harness_fail "autofix_existing backup cleans partial dir after copy failure" "$output"
